---
title: "AI for UVM Economics"
subtitle: "AI tools for teaching and research"
page-layout: full
body-classes: landing-page
title-block-style: none
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<section class="landing-hero-split">
  <div class="hero-left">
    <h1>AI for UVM<br>Economics</h1>
    <p class="hero-subtitle">AI tools for teaching and research</p>
    <p class="hero-meta">Erkmen G. Aslim &amp; Emily Beam</p>
    <p class="hero-meta">Department of Economics · Spring 2026</p>
  </div>
  <div class="hero-right">
    <h2 class="schedule-heading">Bootcamp Schedule</h2>
    <div class="schedule-item">
      <div class="schedule-date">Apr 22</div>
      <div class="schedule-title">Getting Started with AI Tools</div>
      <div class="schedule-detail">11:00–12:30 · Old Mill A500</div>
      <a href="Session1_Slides/session_1_slides.pdf" class="slides-btn">Download slides ↓</a>
    </div>
    <div class="schedule-item">
      <div class="schedule-date">Apr 27</div>
      <div class="schedule-title">Research Pipelines &amp; Automation</div>
      <div class="schedule-detail">11:00–12:30 · Old Mill A500</div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>
```

::: {.landing-content}

[Before You Arrive]{.landing-section-title}

::: {.prep-note}
No installation needed — both sessions are watch-along demos. See the [Getting Set Up](prep.qmd) guide if you want to try things on your own afterward. For broader reading, see [Outside Resources](resources.qmd).
:::

[Session 1: Getting Started with AI Tools]{.landing-section-title}

**April 22, 11:00--12:30, Old Mill A500** — This is a watch-along session. No installation needed.

| Deck | Format |
|------|--------|
| Session 1: Agentic AI Bootcamp (Beam & Aslim) | [PDF](Session1_Slides/session_1_slides.pdf) |

#### What we'll cover

- What AI tools exist and the difference between chat-based and agentic tools
- Context windows, standing instructions, and planning — the key concepts
- What we've actually used these for (and what was fun but saved zero time)
- Voice files: making AI output sound like you
- Skills: reusable workflows you can share
- Live demos: website redesign and custom skill creation

#### Before next time (April 27)

- [ ] Try using an AI tool for one real task this week
- [ ] Install the tools from the [prep guide](prep.qmd) if you haven't already
- [ ] Think of a research or teaching task you'd like to automate

[Session 2: AI for Research and Advanced Teaching]{.landing-section-title}

**April 27, 11:00--12:30, Old Mill A500** — Chaining steps into workflows: research pipelines, slide creation, teaching applications, and automation.

*Slides will be posted here after the session.*

#### What we'll cover

- How to chain AI steps into a documented workflow (plan, execute, review, clear, repeat)
- Creating slides from rough notes
- Teaching applications: assignments, rubrics, study guides
- Research pipeline: raw CPS data to analysis plan to Stata code to report
- Web scraping and automated grading (Erkmen)

#### What to try next

- **Teaching**: Draft a new assignment, create practice problems, or build a study guide
- **Research**: Try the data exploration → analysis plan workflow on your own data
- **Writing**: Use Claude Code to revise a draft, check references, or restructure a section

:::
